meta.title: Jorge Arce — Oral Surgeon · v8 Luke
meta.description: Quiet surgeon, restoring what matters, through precision, patience and care. South Bay, CA.

sections.intro:
Quiet surgeon, restoring what matters,
through precision, patience and care.

sections.text: As an <s>oral surgeon</s>, I craft tailor-made <s>reconstructions</s> for the patients who were told it couldn't be done.
sections.cred: Dr. Jorge Arce — Board certified, trained in Colombia, built in California

rows.s: All-On-X — a complete new set of teeth, planned and placed with precision.
The difficult surgeries other clinicians prefer to refer elsewhere.
From a single implant to a full arch — twenty-seven years of placement.
Rebuilding the foundation where bone and tissue have been lost.
Clarity for complex decisions — including, when right, the advice not to operate.
Diagnosis and treatment for conditions of teeth, gums, bone, and joints.

rows.q: I had been told by two surgeons it was impossible. Dr. Arce took the time no one else did. <s>I can eat, smile, and speak again.</s>
When a case is beyond what I can handle, Jorge is the first call I make. My patients always come back grateful — and so do I.
The most thorough and honest surgeon I have ever met. He treated my father <s>like family.</s>
Calm, precise, and genuinely kind. He explained every step until I understood. The result speaks for itself.
I refer my most complex cases to Dr. Arce without hesitation. <s>Twenty-seven years shows.</s>
Reliable, communicative, and surgically excellent. He sends my patients back with a full report every time.
I was terrified of the procedure. He never rushed me. <s>Best decision I have made for my health.</s>
Years of pain, gone. I only wish I had found him sooner. I recommend Dr. Arce to everyone I know.

rows: When Not to Operate: Judgment in Oral Surgery

posts.d: What full-arch restoration actually involves, who it's for, and what to expect at each stage.

posts.t: How to know if you need an implant
posts.d: The signs worth paying attention to — and why timing often matters more than the procedure itself.
Separating fear from fact, so you can make a calm, informed decision about your health.

sections.note: Taking new patients and referrals — <s>consultations by appointment.</s> Each case is a chance to restore something that matters: bring your scans, your referral letter, or just your questions.
